agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core lifecycle operation in logistics/courier systems. A product manager has requested that users be able to cancel their orders, but the requirement lacks specificity on cancellation windows, eligible states, refund policies, and partial cancellation rules. In courier/logistics, cancellation complexity scales with how far an order has progressed through fulfillment.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A request by a user for pickup and delivery of goods, progressing through a defined lifecycle of states."},{"term":"Order Cancellation","definition":"A user-initiated action that terminates an active order, halting further fulfillment processing."},{"term":"Order State","definition":"The current phase of an order in its lifecycle (e.g., placed, confirmed, assigned, picked up, in transit, delivered)."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period or set of order states during which cancellation is permitted."},{"term":"Cancellation Fee","definition":"A potential charge applied when cancellation occurs after resources have been committed (e.g., courier dispatched)."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the user following a successful cancellation."},{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery agent assigned to fulfill the order."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order can only be cancelled while it is in a cancellable state. Once an order reaches a terminal state (delivered, already cancelled), cancellation is not permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing cancellation of a delivered or already-cancelled order would corrupt order state and financial records. This is a state-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in state 'delivered' or 'cancelled', when a cancellation request is submitted, then the system must reject it."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A cancellation request must be initiated by the user who owns the order, or by an authorized system actor (e.g., admin, support agent).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary actors to cancel orders violates data ownership and could be exploited. This is an access-control integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancellation request from a user who does not own the order and is not an admin, then the system must reject the request."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"When an order is cancelled, all downstream fulfillment operations for that order must cease.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Continuing to fulfill a cancelled order wastes resources and creates billing inconsistencies. State propagation is a system-integrity concern.","testable_assertion":"Given an order that transitions to 'cancelled', then no further state transitions (e.g., picked_up, in_transit) are permitted for that order."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancellation fee may apply depending on how far the order has progressed in fulfillment (e.g., courier already dispatched).","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Fee schedules are a business policy decision that can change. A VP could revise the fee tiers at any time.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with a courier already assigned, when the user cancels, then the applicable cancellation fee (if any) is calculated and communicated before confirmation."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Refund processing must be triggered upon successful cancellation, with the refund amount adjusted for any applicable cancellation fees.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund policies (full vs. partial, timing) are business policy, not state integrity constraints.","testable_assertion":"Given a successfully cancelled order, then a refund record is created with amount equal to (order total minus cancellation fee)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An order exists in state 'placed' or 'confirmed' (before courier assignment)","when":"The owning user requests cancellation","then":"The order transitions to 'cancelled', no cancellation fee is applied, and a full refund is initiated."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An order exists in state 'assigned' (courier dispatched but pickup not yet completed)","when":"The owning user requests cancellation","then":"The order transitions to 'cancelled', the courier is notified of the cancellation, and any applicable cancellation fee is deducted from the refund."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An order exists in state 'picked_up' or 'in_transit'","when":"The owning user requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the cancellation with a clear message explaining why (goods are already in courier possession), OR applies a late-cancellation policy — this behavior requires clarification from PM."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An order exists in a terminal state ('delivered' or 'cancelled')","when":"Any user requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the request and returns an error indicating the order is not in a cancellable state."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A user is authenticated but does not own the order","when":"The user requests cancellation of that order","then":"The system rejects the request with an authorization error."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An order is in a cancellable state and the user confirms cancellation","when":"The cancellation is processed","then":"The order state becomes 'cancelled', a timestamped cancellation event is recorded, the user receives confirmation, and refund processing begins."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A cancellation is requested for an order with a cancellation fee","when":"The user initiates cancellation","then":"The fee amount and adjusted refund are displayed to the user BEFORE they confirm, allowing them to abort the cancellation."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"Multiple rapid cancellation requests are submitted for the same order","when":"The system processes them","then":"Only one cancellation is processed; subsequent requests are idempotently acknowledged without duplicate refunds or fee charges."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"User cancels an order at the exact moment a courier is being assigned (race condition between assignment and cancellation).","expected_behavior":"The system must handle this atomically: either the assignment completes and cancellation is processed under 'assigned' rules (with courier notification), or cancellation wins and assignment is rolled back. No orphaned courier assignments."},{"scenario":"Payment authorization has expired by the time refund is processed.","expected_behavior":"The system must handle refund via the original payment method or fall back to an alternative refund mechanism (e.g., account credit), and notify the user of the refund method used."},{"scenario":"User cancels, then immediately places an identical new order.","expected_behavior":"The new order is treated as a completely independent order. No state leakage from the cancelled order. Cancelled order remains cancelled."},{"scenario":"Network failure occurs mid-cancellation — user confirmed but server did not persist the state change.","expected_behavior":"The system must not leave the order in an ambiguous state. Either the cancellation is retried and completed, or the user is clearly informed the cancellation did not succeed and can retry."},{"scenario":"Order has a promotional discount or coupon applied.","expected_behavior":"Refund policy for promotional orders must be defined — does the user get the discounted amount back, or the full amount? Coupon reinstatement rules must be specified."},{"scenario":"Partial order cancellation — user wants to cancel one item from a multi-item order.","expected_behavior":"Behavior is undefined in the current spec. If supported, recalculate totals and fees; if not supported, reject with a clear message. Requires PM clarification."},{"scenario":"Admin or support agent cancels on behalf of user.","expected_behavior":"Cancellation proceeds with an audit trail indicating the acting agent, and the user is notified that their order was cancelled by support."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested during a system-wide outage or degraded state.","expected_behavior":"Cancellation request must be queued or the user informed to retry. The order must not silently continue fulfillment while the user believes it is cancelled."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation fees offset sunk costs from dispatched couriers. Overly generous cancellation policies reduce revenue; overly strict policies drive user churn. Refund timing affects cash flow. Duplicate-refund bugs could cause direct financial loss.","operational":"Courier dispatch cancellation reduces wasted trips and improves fleet utilization. Clear cancellation states prevent downstream fulfillment errors. Idempotent processing prevents operations team from handling duplicate-refund escalations.","user":"Cancellation capability is a baseline user expectation. A smooth, transparent cancellation flow (with clear fee communication before confirmation) builds trust. Blocking cancellation too aggressively harms user satisfaction; allowing it too late creates logistical chaos.","risk":"Race conditions between cancellation and fulfillment state transitions are the primary technical risk. Financial risk from duplicate refunds or missing cancellation fees. Reputational risk if users cannot cancel orders they believe should be cancellable.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Order Placers)","impact":"Gain ability to cancel orders; experience depends on clarity of cancellation window, fee transparency, and refund speed."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Must receive timely cancellation notifications to avoid wasted pickups. Late cancellations after pickup create logistical burden."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Clear cancellation rules reduce support tickets. Ambiguous edge cases (e.g., mid-transit cancellation) will generate escalations."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Refund processing, cancellation fee reconciliation, and promotional coupon reinstatement all affect financial reporting."},{"group":"Product Management","impact":"Must clarify cancellation window, fee schedule, partial-cancellation support, and promotional order policies to unblock implementation."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec is a single sentence with no detail on cancellation windows, fee structures, eligible states, refund policies, or partial cancellation. Nearly every critical business decision requires PM clarification. The rules and criteria above are based on standard logistics domain patterns, but the actual business policy is unspecified.","high_confidence_areas":["Terminal states (delivered, cancelled) must reject cancellation — this is a universal invariant","Ownership/authorization is required for cancellation","Cancellation must halt downstream fulfillment","Idempotency is required to prevent duplicate refunds"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact set of cancellable states (especially picked_up and in_transit)","Cancellation fee schedule and tier structure","Refund timing and method policies","Whether partial order cancellation is in scope","Promotional order and coupon reinstatement rules"],"assumptions":["Order lifecycle includes at minimum: placed, confirmed, assigned, picked_up, in_transit, delivered, cancelled","Cancellation is user-initiated (not system-initiated or courier-initiated, which would be separate flows)","Payment has been captured or authorized at the time of cancellation","The system has a single order owner (not shared/group orders)"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Which order states are cancellable? The spec says 'cancel their order' but does not define whether cancellation is allowed after courier assignment, after pickup, or during transit. Each boundary has different operational and financial implications.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Couriers","Customer Support","Finance"],"options":["Allow cancellation only before courier assignment (conservative)","Allow cancellation up to pickup (moderate)","Allow cancellation at any pre-delivery state with escalating fees (permissive)"],"recommendation":"Define explicit state-based cancellation rules. Recommend moderate approach: free cancellation before assignment, fee-based cancellation after assignment but before pickup, no cancellation after pickup."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"What is the cancellation fee structure? The spec does not mention fees at all. In logistics, cancellation after courier dispatch incurs real costs.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance","Product Management"],"options":["No cancellation fees ever (simplest, highest cost absorption)","Flat fee after courier assignment","Percentage-based fee scaling with fulfillment progress","Dynamic fee based on courier distance traveled"],"recommendation":"Implement a simple tiered model: no fee before assignment, flat fee after assignment. Revisit with dynamic pricing later based on data."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Is partial order cancellation (cancelling individual items from a multi-item order) in scope?","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Product Management","Finance"],"options":["Only full-order cancellation in v1","Support partial cancellation from day one"],"recommendation":"Scope v1 to full-order cancellation only. Partial cancellation introduces significant complexity in pricing recalculation and should be a separate feature."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"What is the refund policy? Full refund always, or adjusted based on cancellation timing? What payment method is used for refunds? What is the refund SLA?","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance","Customer Support"],"options":["Immediate full refund to original payment method","Refund minus cancellation fee, processed within N business days","Account credit instead of payment refund"],"recommendation":"Refund to original payment method, minus any applicable cancellation fee, initiated immediately upon cancellation confirmation. Define an SLA (e.g., refund visible within 5-10 business days depending on payment provider)."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core lifecycle operation in logistics/courier systems. A product manager has requested that users be able to cancel their orders, but the requirement lacks specificity on cancellation windows, eligible states, refund policies, and partial cancellation rules. In courier/logistics, cancellation complexity scales with how far an order has progressed through fulfillment.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"ter

content.thinking:
The PM says "Users should be able to cancel their order." This is vague - no details about order states, time windows, refund policies, partial cancellations, etc. This is ambiguity.

Let me produce a thorough domain analysis with escalations typed as "ambiguity" since the requirement is vaguely stated but not missing entirely.
